title: Market Brief — Wednesday, April 15, 2026
summary: Compute is tight. H100 availability sits at 16% (111/336 GPUs) — severe shortage territory — while H200 is even worse at 11% (65/341), the scarcest class on the board. B200 is the outlier at 33% (211/500), loosening fast after a 20pp utilization drop in 30 days. The day's biggest move: H200 spot ripped 27.3% to $3.61/hr, signaling acute demand against vanishing supply.
paragraphs: Compute is tight. H100 availability sits at 16% (111/336 GPUs) — severe shortage territory — while H200 is even worse at 11% (65/341), the scarcest class on the board. B200 is the outlier at 33% (211/500), loosening fast after a 20pp utilization drop in 30 days. The day's biggest move: H200 spot ripped 27.3% to $3.61/hr, signaling acute demand against vanishing supply.

B200 is the only GPU where mid-tier inference turns a profit: TCO-adjusted cost on Llama 70B is $3.54/M tokens, giving medium-tier operators a razor-thin 6% margin. H100 and H200 medium-tier operators are underwater, with H200 losing $2.26/M tokens at a negative 131% margin — anyone running mid-price inference on H200s is burning cash. Provider grades: DeepSeek Strong, Meta Strong, OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, xAI Weak.

B200 compute is overvalued at $3.88/hr spot versus $4.22 OCPI with 95.6% annualized volatility and collapsing utilization — short B200 exposure before oversupply reprices it further. H100s at $1.87/hr spot against $1.77 OCPI with only 18.3% volatility and severe scarcity are undervalued for locked-in inference workloads — go long H100 reserved capacity now.
bullets: 11% of H200 GPUs are available (65 of 341), making it the most supply-constrained accelerator class today.
33% of B200 GPUs sit idle (211 of 500), the only class approaching oversupply after a 20.3pp utilization drop in 30 days.
$0.38 per million output tokens is DeepSeek V3.2's price, 99.4% below GPT-4's launch-era $60 benchmark.
$80 per million output tokens makes o3 Pro the most expensive model on the board, 210x pricier than DeepSeek V3.2.
42.8% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest legacy GPU to hold as a capacity position.
21.41 is the current LLMflation index, meaning API pricing has deflated roughly 79% since GPT-4's March 2023 launch.
